summary: Art movement of contemporary provocateurs from the United Kingdom, including Damien Hirst, Tracey Emin, Chris Ofili, Yinka Shonibare, Sarah Lucas, Jenny Saville. Patronized by Charles Saatchi. Exhibitions: Sensation, Freeze. Opposed by Stuckists.

works.name: General
works.description: Movement of provocative contemporary British artists. Key members: Damien Hirst (shark in formaldehyde, diamond skull For the Love of God), Tracey Emin (My Bed, Everyone I Have Ever Slept With tent), Chris Ofili (Holy Virgin Mary with elephant dung, The Upper Room), Yinka Shonibare (Nelson's Ship in a Bottle), Sarah Lucas (food self-portraits), Jenny Saville (large nudes of obese women), Sam Taylor-Johnson, the Chapman Brothers, Rachel Whiteread, Michael Landy, Gary Hume. Patronized by Charles Saatchi. Exhibitions: Freeze (1988, curated by Hirst), Sensation (1997, at Royal Academy). Jay Jopling founded White Cube gallery. Opposed by Stuckist movement (Billy Childish). Turner Prize controversies.

clues.clue: Damien Hirst: shark in formaldehyde (Physical Impossibility of Death); diamond-covered skull (For the Love of God); polka dot paintings burned for NFTs
examples: an artist who displayed a dead shark in formaldehyde
One artist from this country covered a cast of a skull in diamonds in For the Love of God

examples: This movement includes the artist of My Bed

examples: The Chinese performance duo Mad for Real jumped on an artwork from this country consisting of a disheveled bed

comprehensive_summary:
The Young British Artists (YBA) are a movement of provocative contemporary British artists who emerged in the late 1980s, patronized by Charles Saatchi and centered around exhibitions like Freeze (1988, curated by Damien Hirst) and Sensation (1997).

Damien Hirst is the most well-known member, famous for preserving a shark in formaldehyde (The Physical Impossibility of Death in the Mind of Someone Living), covering a skull in diamonds (For the Love of God), and his polka dot paintings. Tracey Emin is known for My Bed (disheveled bed with bloody underwear, jumped on by the duo Mad for Real) and Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995 (tent with names appliqued inside). Chris Ofili's Holy Virgin Mary used elephant dung and angered Rudy Giuliani; his The Upper Room features 13 paintings of rhesus macaques.

Other members include Yinka Shonibare (headless mannequins in Ankara fabric), Sarah Lucas (food-themed self-portraits), Jenny Saville (large nudes of obese women, Propped with mirror-written Luce Irigaray text), the Chapman Brothers, Rachel Whiteread, Sam Taylor-Johnson, Michael Landy, and Gary Hume. Jay Jopling founded the White Cube gallery. The Stuckist movement (Billy Childish) was created to oppose them. Multiple members were controversially nominated for or won the Turner Prize.

cards.front: Movement: Damien Hirst preserved a shark in formaldehyde; Tracey Emin displayed My Bed; patronized by Charles Saatchi; Freeze and Sensation exhibitions
Movement: Chris Ofili used elephant dung in Holy Virgin Mary (angered Giuliani) and painted 13 rhesus macaques for The Upper Room
Movement: opposed by Stuckist movement founded by Billy Childish; Jay Jopling founded White Cube gallery
Movement: Jenny Saville known for large nudes of obese women; Sarah Lucas for food-themed self-portraits like Eating a Banana
